content.body: re PR #3885, I think we need an ecmarkup lint to detect when a section should have a `type` attribute but doesn't. Problem is detecting when it should. One heuristic: if it directly contains an `<emu-alg>`, then it should probably have a `type`. Currently 24 exceptions. If we gave `type` attributes to those 24 (e.g. `type="shorthand"`), then the lint would work.
content.formatted_body: re PR #3885, I think we need an ecmarkup lint to detect when a section should have a type attribute but doesn't. Problem is detecting when it should. One heuristic: if it directly contains an <emu-alg>, then it should probably have a type. Currently 24 exceptions. If we gave type attributes to those 24 (e.g. type="shorthand"), then the lint would work.

content.body: (Or we could give a `type` to every section, but that's a bigger job.)
content.formatted_body: (Or we could give a type to every section, but that's a bigger job.)

content.body: * re PR #3885, I think we need an ecmarkup lint to detect when a section should have a `type` attribute but doesn't. Problem is detecting when it should. If we gave `type` attributes to those 24 (e.g. `type="shorthand"`), then we could use that criterion for the lint.

content.body: I like the spirit, but I don't like adding a `type=` for no reason other than to mark it for exclusion from an ecmarkup heuristic
content.formatted_body: I like the spirit, but I don't like adding a type= for no reason other than to mark it for exclusion from an ecmarkup heuristic

content.body: so make up a better reason then
It's not the *only* reason, it's just the reason that's relevant to 3885.
content.formatted_body: It's not the only reason, it's just the reason that's relevant to 3885.
